name: Hypothalamus
location: Ventral to the thalamus, forming the floor and lower lateral walls of the third ventricle.
description: The master homeostatic command center regulating body temperature, thirst, hunger, circadian rhythms, emotional behavior, and neuroendocrine control via the pituitary gland.
functions: Circadian rhythm control via suprachiasmatic nucleus (SCN)
bloodSupply: Circle of Willis (Anterior Communicating & Internal Carotid)
clinicalImportance: Essential for bodily survival; tiny lesions cause severe metabolic, endocrine, or autonomic failure.
researchNotes: Suprachiasmatic nucleus (SCN) inside the hypothalamus operates as the master molecular circadian clock driven by CLOCK/BMAL1 genes.
interestingFacts: Weighs only 4 grams (0.3% of brain weight) but governs all basic survival drives.
